entry.title: Unable to Change House Bank in Open Item of Advance Request (CME F)
entry.content:
I'm working in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and I have encountered a behavior that I don’t fully understand.

When I create an advance request (CME F), the corresponding open item is generated. The issue is that when I access the individual line item and try to edit it in order to select a different house bank, the system does not allow the change and displays the following message: "It is not possible to change House Bank for line item".

My question is: Should it be possible to change the house bank in the open item of an advance request? Why does the system prevent changing the house bank?

I would appreciate if someone could confirm whether this is a standard limitation of the CME F process in Public Cloud.

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: Connecting SAP GenAI Hub to n8n: A Complete Guide
entry.content:
A step-by-step tutorial on calling SAP GenAI Hub's large language models from self-hosted n8n to build enterprise AI automation workflows.

Why This Combination?

If your organization uses the SAP ecosystem and wants to build flexible automation workflows, n8n + SAP GenAI Hub is a powerful combination worth exploring:

  • n8n Community Edition: Open-source, self-hostable, no execution limits
  • SAP GenAI Hub: Enterprise-grade compliance, unified AI model access, supports multiple mainstream LLMs

However, n8n doesn't have a native SAP GenAI Hub node yet. No worries — we can easily make the connection using HTTP Request + OAuth2.

Prerequisites

Before we start, make sure you have:

  1. A running n8n instance (Docker or npm or k8s)
  2. SAP BTP account with SAP AI Core service enabled (Extended Plan for GenAI Hub)
  3. A deployed LLM model (configured in SAP AI Launchpad)

 

Get Your SAP AI Core Service Key

Navigate to SAP BTP Cockpit → Your Subaccount → Instances and Subscriptions → SAP AI Core → Create Service Key

You'll get a JSON like this:

 
{
  "clientid": "sb-xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx!b12345|aicore!b540",
  "clientsecret": "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx$xxxxxxxxx",
  "url": "https://your-tenant.authentication.eu10.hana.ondemand.com",
  "serviceurls": {
    "AI_API_URL": "https://api.ai.prod.eu-central-1.aws.ml.hana.ondemand.com"
  }
}

Keep these four fields handy — you'll need them all.

 

Get Your Deployment ID

In SAP AI Launchpad:

  1. Go to ML Operations → Deployments
  2. Find your deployed model
  3. Note the Deployment ID (or the full deploymentUrl)

Step 1: Create OAuth2 Credential

SAP GenAI Hub uses OAuth2 Client Credentials authentication. Let's configure the credential in n8n first.

  1. Open n8n, go to Settings → Credentials
  2. Click Add Credential, search for OAuth2 API
  3. Fill in the following:
Field
Value
Credential NameSAP-GenAI-Hub
Grant TypeClient Credentials
Access Token URLhttps://your-tenant.authentication.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token
Client IDCopy the full clientid from Service Key
Client SecretCopy the full clientsecret from Service Key
ScopeLeave empty
AuthenticationSelect Header
4. Click Save

💡Common Mistake: Selecting "Send as Basic Auth Header" will cause 401 authentication failures. SAP requires credentials to be sent in the body.

Step 2: Build a Simple Q&A Workflow

Let's create a basic LLM query workflow.

2.1 Add Manual Trigger

Start with a manual trigger for testing:

  • Add a Manual Trigger node

2.2 Add HTTP Request Node

This is the core node. Configure as follows:

Basic Settings:

  • Method: POST
  • URL: 
https://api.ai.prod.eu-central-1.aws.ml.hana.ondemand.com/v2/inference/deployments/{your_Deployment_ID}/chat/completions​

 ⚠️ Note: If using OpenAI models, you should add the api-version parameter, e.g.

https://api.ai.prod.eu-central-1.aws.ml.hana.ondemand.com/v2/inference/deployments/{your_Deployment_ID}/chat/completions?api-version=2023-05-15

Authentication:

  • Authentication: Predefined Credential Type
  • Credential Type: OAuth2 API
  • OAuth2 API: Select SAP-GenAI-Hub

Headers:

NameValue
Content-Typeapplication/json
AI-Resource-Groupdefault, or your own Resource Group ID

Body:

  • Body Content Type: JSON
  • Specify Body: Using JSON
{
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "system",
      "content": "You are a professional assistant. Please provide helpful and accurate answers."
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "What is SAP S/4HANA? Please summarize in three sentences."
    }
  ]
}

2.3 Test It

Click Test Workflow. If configured correctly, you'll see a response like:

{
  "choices": [
    {
      "message": {
        "role": "assistant",
        "content": "SAP S/4HANA is SAP's next-generation intelligent ERP suite..."
      }
    }
  ],
  "usage": {
    "prompt_tokens": 45,
    "completion_tokens": 128,
    "total_tokens": 173
  }
}

🎉Congratulations! You've successfully called SAP GenAI Hub from n8n!

Step 3: Build an Interactive Chat Workflow

Let's upgrade to a conversational version.

Workflow Structure

Chat Trigger → HTTP Request (SAP GenAI Hub) → Code (Extract Reply)

3.1 Chat Trigger Node

Add a Chat Trigger node (found under Advanced AI category):

  • Keep default settings
  • It provides an embedded chat interface for testing

3.2 Modify HTTP Request Node

Change the Body to dynamically get user input:

{
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "system",
      "content": "You are a professional enterprise consultant specializing in SAP and digital transformation topics."
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "{{ $json.chatInput }}"
    }
  ]
}

{{ $json.chatInput }} automatically retrieves the user's input from the chat interface.

3.3 Add Code Node to Extract Response

Add a Code node to extract the AI's reply:

const response = $input.all()[0].json;
const aiMessage = response.choices?.[0]?.message?.content || "Sorry, no response received.";

return [{
  json: {
    response: aiMessage  }
}];

3.4 Test the Chat

  1. Activate the Workflow
  2. Click the Chat button to open the chat interface
  3. Start chatting!

Step 4: Add Conversation Memory (Optional)

The above version treats each message independently without context. We can use n8n's Workflow Static Data to implement simple memory.

Improved Workflow

Add a Code node before HTTP Request to manage conversation history:

// Get session ID and user input
const sessionId = $json.sessionId || 'default';
const userInput = $json.chatInput;

// Get history from global static data
const staticData = $getWorkflowStaticData('global');
if (!staticData.conversations) {
  staticData.conversations = {};
}
if (!staticData.conversations[sessionId]) {
  staticData.conversations[sessionId] = [
    {
      role: "system",
      content: "You are a professional enterprise consultant specializing in SAP and digital transformation topics."
    }
  ];
}

// Add user message
staticData.conversations[sessionId].push({
  role: "user",
  content: userInput});

// Keep only the last 20 messages (avoid token limit)
const messages = staticData.conversations[sessionId].slice(-20);

return [{
  json: {
    sessionId,
    messages  }
}];

Then modify the HTTP Request Body:

{
  "messages": {{ JSON.stringify($json.messages) }}
}

Finally, save the assistant's reply in the response extraction Code node:

const response = $input.all()[0].json;
const sessionId = $('Prepare Messages').first().json.sessionId;
const aiMessage = response.choices?.[0]?.message?.content || "Sorry, no response received.";

// Save assistant reply to history
const staticData = $getWorkflowStaticData('global');
staticData.conversations[sessionId].push({
  role: "assistant",
  content: aiMessage});

return [{
  json: {
    response: aiMessage  }
}];

Now your chatbot remembers context!

Practical Use Cases

Use Case 1: Automated Email Replies

Email Trigger → Extract Content → SAP GenAI Hub → Send Reply

Sample Prompt:

 
Based on the following customer email, generate a professional reply:
Email content:{{ $json.emailBody }}
Requirements:1. Professional and friendly tone2. If it's a price inquiry, mention that detailed pricing will follow within 24 hours3. If it's a complaint, express apology and commitment to follow up

Use Case 2: Document Summarization

Webhook → Read PDF → SAP GenAI Hub → Save to Notion

 

Use Case 3: Data Analysis Assistant

Combine SAP APIs to fetch business data and let AI generate analysis reports:

Schedule Trigger → SAP S/4HANA → SAP GenAI Hub → Slack Message

Troubleshooting

❌401 Unauthorized

Cause: Authentication failed

Check:

  • Client ID and Secret are fully copied (including special characters like !, |, $)
  • Authentication is set to Send In Body
  • Token URL format is correct (ends with /oauth/token)

❌404 Not Found

Cause: API path error

Check:

  • Deployment ID is correct
  • Model is successfully deployed (status shows "Running" in AI Launchpad)
  • API URL path format: /v2/inference/deployments/{id}/chat/completions

❌403 Forbidden

Cause: Insufficient permissions

Check:

  • AI-Resource-Group header is set (default is default)
  • User has permission to access the deployment

❌400 Bad Request

Cause: Invalid request body

Check:

  • JSON format is correct
  • messages is an array
  • Each message has both role and content

Optimization Tips

  1. Limit conversation history: Keep only the last N turns to avoid exceeding context window
  2. Add error handling: Use n8n's Error Trigger to handle API failures gracefully
  3. Use variables: Store API URLs and Deployment IDs in n8n Variables for easier management

Summary

In this tutorial, you learned:

✅How to configure OAuth2 authentication for SAP GenAI Hub in n8n
✅How to use HTTP Request node to call LLM APIs
✅How to build a chatbot with conversation memory
✅How to troubleshoot common errors

The power of n8n lies in its flexibility — you can seamlessly integrate SAP GenAI Hub with hundreds of other applications (Email, Databases, SAP S/4HANA, etc.) to build truly enterprise-grade AI automation workflows.

Happy Automating! 🚀

entry.title: Declarative UI Integration Cards - Consistent Framework With Flexible Content Capabilities
entry.content:
Introduction

Since the early announcement of the UI Integration Cards paradigm (as described in this article), we have been busy expanding the functionality of Cards, resulting in an ever-growing adoption across almost all SAP products.

Today, Cards are utilized in a range of solutions and scenarios, from the SAP S/4HANA home page with “To-Do” and Insights Cards, to SAP Build Work Zone, where Cards from any SAP product can be easily assembled together, to Joule, and also on mobile-native screens. 

 

 

      

The fundamental principles that underpin this technology, as detailed on our Card Explorer landing page, are that it is designed to be Declarative, Easy to consume, and Consistent.

  1. Declarative: configured by a manifest.json                              ==> Low to No-code skills required (AI-gen@UI5 MCP server)
  2. Easy to consume: product and UI-technology agnostic      ==> Mobile-ready / Integration-ready via configuration
  3. Consistent: predefined card types in Fiori/Horizon design ==> Thoughtfully designed / Product Standards covered

Key Concepts and Capabilities

In this article, we’ll detail the key qualities and advantages of the Declarative Integration Cards. Declarative Card types offer a range of display options and minimalist interactions, including List Card, Table Card, Timeline, Calendar, Analytical, and Object Card with input elements.

Declarative Card types enable the fast and easy creation of “Home page” and “Overview” pages, where Card developers can integrate data from various sources to display relevant business data for any SAP or non-SAP product.

Declarative and Consistent

Integration Cards are straightforwardly defined using a single JSON descriptor file called manifest.json. This file is all you need to get a fully functional and nicely rendered representation of business data in the form of a Card. Once you have this descriptive manifest.json file, our Card Runtime can process it and render the card in a web browser or any mobile-native device, regardless of the UI framework. Hence – declarative!  

Anatomy of Declarative UI Integration Cards

Examining the Card's structure, we can identify three separate parts:

The Card Header provides the card's identity, typically represented by a title, subtitle, and icon or avatar/image attributes. It can also display more dynamic or complex data, such as the special Numeric Header, which prominently features a key numeric indicator, or an extended Header.

  • The Content / Data is the main part of the card that displays relevant business data in a selected type of view. These data types can be List, Table, Object, Analytical, Timeline, or Calendar.
  • The Footer area is usually designated for intended Card interaction elements, such as action buttons or links for navigation.

The general structure of a declarative manifest.json looks like this: 

 

{
  "sap.app": {
    "id": "my.card.id",
    "type": "card",
    "applicationVersion": {
      "version": "1.0.0"
    }
  },
  "sap.card": {
    "type": "List",  // Card type: List, Table, Object, Analytical, Timeline, Calendar
    "header": {
      // Header configuration
    },
    "content": {
      // Content configuration specific to card type
    },
    "footer": {
      // Optional footer with actions
    }
  }
}

 

The Card Header

The card header displays essential information about the card by grouping a set of card attributes and presenting them at the top of the card. The "sap.card"/header section is mandatory. Providing it together with the title is essential for proper screen reader support and other functionalities.

Integration Cards support three header configurations:

  1. Default Header - Standard header with title, subtitle, icon, status
  2. Numeric Header - KPI-focused header with prominent numeric indicators
  3. Extended Header with Info Section - flexible layout within either kind of header, capable of accommodating various components

All headers support actions, data timestamps, visibility control, and text wrapping options. 

Default Header

The standard header displays general information about the card. 

 

"header": {
	"title": "An example title",
	"subtitle": "Some subtitle",
	"icon": {
		"src": "sap-icon://business-objects-experience",
		"visible": true
	},
	"status": {
		"text": "5 of 20"
	}
}

 


Numeric Header

The Numeric Header is optimized for displaying KPIs, metrics, and quantitative data with prominent visualization. 

 

"header": {
	"type": "Numeric",
	"title": "Project Cloud Transformation",
	"subtitle": "Revenue",
	"unitOfMeasurement": "EUR",
	"mainIndicator": {
		"number": "44",
		"unit": "%",
		"trend": "Down",
		"state": "Critical"
	},
	"details": "Some details",
	"sideIndicators": [
		{
			"title": "Target",
			"number": "17",
			"unit": "%"
		},
		{
			"title": "Deviation",
			"number": "5",
			"unit": "%"
		}
	]
}

 


Extended Header with Info Section

Both header types can be extended with an info section for additional grouped information.

Currently, only items of type "Status" are available.

 

"header": {
	"title": "Project Cloud Transformation",
	"subtitle": "Revenue",
	"infoSection": {
		"rows": [
			{
				"items": [
					{
						"type": "Status",
						"value": "On Track",
						"state": "Success",
						"inverted": true,
						"showStateIcon": true
					},
					{
						"type": "Status",
						"value": "OKR Relevant",
						"state": "None",
						"inverted": true,
						"showStateIcon": true
					},
					…
				]
			}
		]
	}
}

 

The Card Content Area

The Card content area defines how data is rendered by selecting a declarative card type, each offering predefined structures and configuration options to visualize business information in a consistent and self-contained way.

  • List Card – Displays multiple items in a vertical list with flexible layouts to present titles, descriptions, and multiple attributes per item.
  • Table Card – Visualizes structured data in rows and columns with configurable formatting, alignment, grouping, and item count to support clear data comparison and summarization.
  • Object Card – Presents information about a single object in configurable groups, supporting rich attribute types, visibility control, and consistent usage across SAP products.
  • Analytical Card – Displays analytical business data using chart visualizations with defined dimensions to ensure accurate and meaningful insight representation.
  • Timeline Card – Shows time-related information as a sequence of events arranged chronologically with clear visual flow and contextual details.
  • Calendar Card – Displays agenda or timetable data for an entity with interactive date selection, semantic legends, and dynamic data updates.

Stay tuned for future articles with details for every Card type. 

The Card Footer

The card footer, located at the bottom of the card, is used for important or routine actions that directly impact the card functionality, such as "Approve" or "Submit" actions. It serves as the designated area where users can interact with or respond to the card's content through an Actions Strip—a flexible toolbar that accommodates a variety of interactive elements.

The Actions Strip supports diverse interaction patterns through three primary element types: Buttons for actionable operations, Links for navigation purposes, and Labels for displaying contextual information. Additionally, ToolbarSpacer elements provide precise control over the visual arrangement of actions, enabling left-aligned, right-aligned, or centered positioning within the footer.

Each action element can be extensively configured to match specific use cases. Buttons offer multiple visual styles (Accept, Reject, Transparent, Emphasized) and can display either text, icons, or both—with the ability to prefer icon-only display in space-constrained layouts. All elements support standard properties including tooltips, visibility conditions, accessibility attributes, and overflow behavior through overflowPriority settings (High, Low, NeverOverflow, AlwaysOverflow), which determine how actions adapt to different screen sizes.

Actions themselves can be of various types: Navigation actions for opening URLs or triggering deep links, Submit actions for posting data to backend services, Custom actions that invoke extension methods. Each action can be configured with parameters and enabled/disabled states, often bound to dynamic data for context-sensitive behavior.

Here's an example demonstrating the versatility of footer configurations:

 

"footer": {
  "actionsStrip": [
    {
      "type": "Link",
      "text": "View Details",
      "icon": "sap-icon://detail-view",
      "actions": [
        {
          "type": "Navigation",
          "parameters": {
            "url": "{detailsUrl}"
          }
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "type": "ToolbarSpacer"
    },
    {
      "text": "Approve",
      "buttonType": "Accept",
      "overflowPriority": "High",
      "actions": [
        {
          "type": "Submit",
          "url": "/api/approve",
          "method": "POST"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "buttonType": "Transparent",
      "icon": "sap-icon://email",
      "preferIcon": true,
      "text": "Contact",
      "tooltip": "Send an email",
      "actions": [
        {
          "type": "Navigation",
          "parameters": {
            "url": "mailto:{company/email}?subject={company/emailSubject}"
          }
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

 

Beyond the Actions Strip, the footer supports additional built-in features that enhance usability. It provides built-in pagination/scrolling support through the "Show More" button, which elegantly handles content overflow by opening cards in an resizable Dialog view. When cards are displayed within dialogs, the footer automatically manages a "Close" button, streamlining the user experience without requiring explicit configuration. This is achieved by defining paginator element in the footer declaration. 

		"footer": {
			"paginator": {
				"totalCount": "{/itemsCount}",
				"pageSize": 5
			}
		}

The footer's visibility can be controlled dynamically through binding expressions, allowing it to appear only when specific conditions are met: 

 

"footer": {
  "visible": "{= ${/itemsCount} > 0}",
  "actionsStrip": [ /* actions configuration */ ]
}

 

Final Thoughts

You can use Declarative UI Integration Cards as an entry point to an app or if you want the user to focus on a single object, topic, or group of objects.

Avoid building complex, small apps within the card that require navigation flows. Instead of being complex or multifunctional, each card is designed to serve a specific role or display business data in a clear manner.

Clarity and simplicity are prioritized in Declarative UI Integration Cards, enabling users to easily understand and integrate the cards without unnecessary complexity or ambiguity.

The architecture's three-part structure—header, content, and footer—establishes a predictable framework that accelerates development while ensuring consistency across diverse use cases. This structured approach not only simplifies the creation process but also enhances the end-user experience through familiar, intuitive patterns that work seamlessly across all SAP solutions.

By embracing the declarative paradigm, developers can rapidly compose powerful, data-driven cards without deep technical expertise, while maintaining the enterprise-grade quality and accessibility standards that users expect. The result is a flexible, scalable solution that transforms how business information is surfaced and acted upon across the digital workplace.

entry.title: RF - Decode barcode: Badi /SCWM/EX_BARC_DECODE not triggered for Serial Number screen
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a development to decode barcodes in EWM.
We use the badi /SCWM/EX_BARC_DECODE and it's working fine.

In the configuration, we ticked the indicator "Badi" in the barcode definition. When we scan a barcode starting by the prefix in the config, the badi is triggered and we decode the barcode.

This works with the fields material and batch but it doesn't work for the serial number (in the standard screen to collect the SN).

Do you have a solution to trigger the badi?

If not, what's the solution to decode a barcode in this screen?


Thanks in advance;

Kr,

entry.title: Re: Schedules for Maintenance Deliveries is moving to SAP for Me
entry.content:
Now the old simple PDF calender is gone.
I would appreciate, if  there would be at least a quarter/half-year/yearly view too (instead of monthly at maximum)
Often  in upgrade planning, somebody  is asking "To which release could we upgrade" and using  filter
"Maintenance deliveries (patches etc.)"  i.e. for SAP S/HANA Release XYZ and scrolling all the months is annoying.

As others already wrote: There might be some reasons for the new calendar, but the simple PDF was MUCH easier for upgrade planning and very easy to  handle, could be sent to collegues etc...
Export function in new calendar (creation of an ics file) is nice for importing into mail program and being reminded , but not helpful for GENERAL upgrade/release planning.

entry.title: Re: C_IEE2E vs. TS410: Is this the new standard for S/4HA...
entry.content:
Thank you for your question!

The C_IEE2E certification is the successor of the C_TS410 certification. It is the recommended entry-level certification that validates SAP end-to-end business process skills. The certification has recently been updated to a system-based exam.

SAP Business Suite unites SAP’s core business applications, data, and AI capabilities. You can learn more in the IEE2E course here: Introducing the SAP Business Suite. So, with C_IEE2E you are perfectly aligned with SAP’s latest strategy and the features and functions of Cloud ERP.

Good luck!

entry.title: SAP CPQ - Multi-Level Line Item Structure
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I have configured a product with a container attribute.

The container property references a configurable product whose attributes are set as Is Line Item.

When I add the main product to the quote, I can't see the third-level items.

I was expecting a structure like this:

1 WBS_Generica

1.1 Componente_WBS_Generica

1.1.1 Acquisto materiale

1.1.2 Trasporti

1.2 Componente_WBS_Generica

1.2.1 Acquisto materiale

1.2.2 Trasporti

However, the 1.1.* and 1.2.* levels are missing.

Does anyone know what might be causing this behavior?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Installation SAP Data Services 4.3
entry.content:
I would like to install SAP Data Services 4.3, and I want to make sure I fully understand the prerequisites before getting started.
So far, I know that I need to:

Download and install the SAP Installation Planner (IPS) to generate the installation files.

Install the Data Services 4.3 Server on the target server.

Install the Data Services 4.3 Client (Designer) on the user workstations.

However, I am confused about the CMS (Central Management Server). I’m not exactly sure what it is:

  1. Is it a standalone product?
  2. Is it part of IPS or the Data Services Server/Client?
  3. Is it mandatory to use SAP Data Services, or only required if one wants to integrate with the SAP BusinessObjects Management Console?

I would like to clarify the role of the CMS in the SAP Data Services 4.3 architecture and understand whether I can install and use DS 4.3 independently for my ETL jobs and database connections without installing the CMS.

Thank you in advance for your clarifications!

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Memory Estimator
entry.content:
For many SAP customers planning their adoption of SAP Cloud ALM, one of the most common questions is: “How much HANA memory can I expect in my SAP Cloud ALM tenant based on my system landscape and daily workload?”

Since every customer operates different systems, workloads, and use cases, the volume of monitoring data collected by SAP Cloud ALM can vary significantly. This makes it essential to base sizing discussions on real, system-specific data rather than generic assumptions.

With the report /SDF/CALM_MEMORY_ESTIMATION, SAP provides a simple way to simulate one full day of SAP Cloud ALM data collection in your ABAP system. The report calculates the number of entities that would have been transferred to SAP Cloud ALM and derives the corresponding estimated HANA memory footprint. When combined with the Excel-based SAP Cloud ALM Memory Estimator tool, customers can aggregate results across multiple systems and model retention times to derive a comprehensive SAP Cloud ALM memory sizing baseline.

This blog post explains step by step how to run the report, export the results, and generate a consolidated memory estimation for your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.

Prerequisites

Before running the estimation, ensure that your system meets the following prerequisites:

  • ST-PI 740 SP32 (or higher)
  • SAP Note 3639977 – Collective corrections as of ST‑PI 7.40 SP32 for SAP Cloud ALM

These components provide the report /SDF/CALM_MEMORY_ESTIMATION, which simulates the data transfer to SAP Cloud ALM.

Step 1: Execute the Memory Estimation Report

  1. Start transaction SE38.
  2. Enter the program: /SDF/CALM_MEMORY_ESTIMATION
  3. Select the option “Execute Estimation Report”.
  4. Choose a representative completed day - ideally a day with typical workload, not a holiday or peak day.
  5. Start the report in the background mode:
    Program → Execute in background

Because the report simulates an entire day of Cloud ALM data collection, it may run for several minutes. You can monitor the background job via SM37.

Step 2: Display the Estimation Results

Once the job finishes:

  1. Click Refresh to update the timestamps.
  2. Select “Display last result”.
  3. Execute the report to display the estimation table.

The output shows:

  • Evaluated use cases
  • Categories of collected entities
  • Number of entities collected during the simulated day
  • Estimated memory consumption (in bytes) per category

This breakdown highlights which scenarios (e.g., Integration Monitoring, Job & Automation Monitoring, Exception Management) have the highest impact on memory usage.

Step 3: Export and Store the Estimation Data

For further analysis and documentation, export the estimation result as a CSV file:

  • Select “Export result as csv file”
  • The filename automatically includes:
    • System SID
    • Client
    • Timestamp

This naming convention ensures traceability when aggregating results from multiple systems.

Analyze the Results

To estimate the total memory usage across your landscape, you can import multiple report outputs into the Excel file SAP_Cloud _ALM_Memory_Estimator.xlsm. You can download the file here.

Open the file and click “Enable Macros” to activate the built‑in calculations.

Import data from the report

  • Click “Import Data”
  • Under “Files to process”, select the exported CSV file
    The system name is automatically taken from the file name
  • Use the “+” button to add more CSV files
  • Click “Finish Import” when all files have been added

Your systems will now appear in the overview table.

Generate the results

Click “Generate Report” to calculate the results. This will switch to the sheet "Memory Estimation Report" where you can see:

  • Estimated memory usage per system
  • Total memory usage across all systems
  • You can also expand the table using the “+” buttons to view the memory footprint per use case

Modify the retention times

Memory usage in SAP Cloud ALM depends on the configured retention times of each application. Longer data retention leads to higher memory needs.

The Excel tool allows you to modify data retention times directly in the header section:

  • Change the retention values as needed
  • Click “Generate Report” again to recalculate the results

Summary

The report /SDF/CALM_MEMORY_ESTIMATION provides SAP customers with a workload‑based method to estimate the memory requirements of SAP Cloud ALM. By simulating a full day of monitoring data collection directly in your connected systems, you gain:

  • Transparency into the monitoring data volume your landscape produces
  • A detailed breakdown of memory consumption across SAP Cloud ALM applications
  • A consolidated memory sizing baseline when combining multiple system results

Together with the Excel-based Memory Estimator, this approach enables a customer‑specific estimation of SAP Cloud ALM storage needs - an essential step when onboarding to SAP Cloud ALM or expanding your monitoring scenarios.

entry.title: Working with SAP SDK for Android and older Android devices with DigiCert G5 Certificate
entry.content:
Background

SAP is changing the root certificate of the domains BTP to DigiCert TLS RSA4096 Root G5 CA, see SAP Note 3566727 for more details.

The new certificate is supported by Android 14 and later. For older devices that cannot be upgraded, the certificate has to be installed on the device or pinned in the Android App. This post explains the application upgrade approach. 

Important: The following approach essentially adds the DigiCert G5 certificate such that the application can validate the SAP Certificate signed by it. As such, great care should be taken to ensure that correct certificate is being added. The DigiCert TLS RSA4096 Root G5 used in the following was downloaded from DigiCert Trusted Root Authority Certificates.

These changes are based on Android Wizard generated App. Depending on your project, different changes may be required. 

Online Functionality Only

1. Download the root certificate, e.g. from https://cacerts.digicert.com/DigiCertTLSRSA4096RootG5.crt mentioned in the above note

2. Rename the file to all lowercase letters, e.g. digicert_tls_rsa_4096_root_g5.crt

3. Copy or move this certificate file to your raw resource folder app/src/main/res/raw/digicert_tls_rsa_4096_root_g5.crt

4. Create or modify the file app/src/main/res/xml/network_security_config.xml and add a reference to the certificate file. In the following example, the App trusts servers that identify with this certificate in addition to all other certificates in the System store.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<network-security-config>
    <base-config>
        <trust-anchors>
            <certificates src="system" />
            <certificates src="@raw/digicert_tls_rsa_4096_root_g5" />
        </trust-anchors>
    </base-config>
</network-security-config>

5. In your android manifest (app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml) locate the application element and add networkSecurityConfig attribute referencing the above config:

<application
    android:name=".app.SAPWizardApplication"
    android:networkSecurityConfig="@xml/network_security_config"
    <!-- other existing content ->
>

Offline Functionality

In addition to above, the following changes are required for apps utilizing the Offline functionality.

For Offline functionality, the certificate file has to be provided as a `trusted_certificates` in `OfflineODataParameters.extraStreamParameters` used for creating `OfflineODataProvider`. In the following example, the Wizard generated code is updated to trust the G5 certificate from the above example.

Locate the code where `OfflineODataParameters` object is created and add `extraStreamParameters`. Ensure that the property is not already assigned, which would overwrite these changes. In that case, concatenate this with existing value.

val offlineODataParameters = OfflineODataParameters().apply {
    storeName = OFFLINE_DATASTORE
    extraStreamParameters = "trusted_certificates=" + ensureTrustedRootsFile(context) + ";"
    ...// other attributes
}

Note the above code is using the function ensureTrustedRootsFile, which can be defined in the same file as follows. It simply creates a file on the device with the contents of the certificate provided in the manifest as a resource.

private fun ensureTrustedRootsFile(context: Context): String {
    val target = File(context.filesDir, "digicert_tls_rsa_4096_root_g5")
    me.g5test.service.OfflineWorkerUtil.logger.error("Checking for cert file")
    me.g5test.service.OfflineWorkerUtil.logger.error(target.absolutePath)
    try {
        if (!target.exists() || target.length() == 0L) {
            context.resources.openRawResource(R.raw.digicert_tls_rsa_4096_root_g5).use { input ->
                FileOutputStream(target).use { output ->
                    input.copyTo(output)
                }
            }
        }
    } catch (e: Exception) {
        me.g5test.service.OfflineWorkerUtil.logger.error("Failed to prepare trusted roots file: ${e.message}")
        throw e
    }
    return target.absolutePath
}

 Sample

Please see hemal-pandya-sap/g5-android: Sample Android project to with old devices domains using G5 certificate for a working sample

Conclusion

As explained above, without any changes the mobile app may not be able to connect after the switch to G5 and result in complete outage. Feel free to post any follow-up questions here.

entry.title: Re: Test data refresh SAP service prior to go live
entry.content:
Good day @phaneste02

If you have the parallel line available, it gives you an additional configuration workspace and two additional tenants for configuration and testing. So using the parallel line quality tenant to run a full migration cockpit mock load is reasonable approach. Blog: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape, parallel line – High Level Introduction also describes this.

However, it does not replace the Test Data Refresh service before you go live, because that service is designed to replace data in a quality system with data from your production system, which assumes your production system is the source.

So, if your goal is to copy Prod to Test before Prod exists, parallel line will not change that.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP Academic Community Conference 2026 | Latin America & the Caribbean | Registrations open!
entry.content:
SAP University Alliances and the SAP Academic Board Ibero-America are pleased to invite you to the SAP Academic Community Conference Latin America & the Caribbean 2026, an inspiring gathering designed especially for SAP University Alliances faculty members and prospects across the region.

In 2026, the conference will bring the Latin America & the Caribbean community together in one shared on-site experience in Brazil, welcoming participants for three days of learning, networking, and collaboration at SAP Labs Latin America in São Leopoldo, one of SAP’s most important innovation hubs in the world.

This edition offers a truly special opportunity to experience first-hand the environment where SAP’s innovation happens. As the primary development hub in Latin America, SAP Labs plays a strategic role in shaping solutions for a diverse and rapidly evolving market. It is where research, technology, and industry needs converge, and where new ideas become real-world innovation.

With a rich agenda of talks, hands-on sessions, and trainings, the conference is designed to support educators with practical insights, spark new ideas for teaching, and create meaningful connections with peers from across the region. Above all, it is a unique chance to strengthen collaboration within our academic network and leave inspired by the people, conversations, and experiences that make this community so special.

Dates: May 19-21, 2026
Location: SAP Labs Latin America – São Leopoldo, Brazil
Registration link: SAP ACC LAC 2026 | Registration Page

This event is free of charge, but seats are limited and registrations will be accepted on a first-come, first-served basis.

Planning your trip? Take a look at our Welcome Guide with helpful information on travel, transportation, and what to expect on-site: Welcome Guide | SAP ACC LAC 2026

We’d love to hear from you! Feel free to leave a comment below and let us know if you’re planning to join us in São Leopoldo.

#SAPACC #SAPUniversityAlliances

entry.title: Manage KPIs and Reports - CDS View contains parameter P_GLAccountHierarchy with / (slash)
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

i created a CDS View (consumption view - define view entity) based on I_MargAnlysProdAndSrvcCube. This Cube requires mandatory parameter P_GLAccountHierarchy to be supplied.

Either a Financial Statement Version (FSV) or GL Account Hierarchy is allowed. I created them in Manage Global Hierarchies App and they can be used and data is returned.

However, if i use annotation @odata.publish: true and @analytics.query: true to make my custom CDS View available as a valid data source in Manage KPIs and Reports App, the then created KPI Tile returns no data if the default value for the hierarchy is set to "H109/HONE/H003".

as select from I_MargAnlysProdAndSrvcCube
              (P_GLAccountHierarchy : 'H109/HONE/H003')

The "path" is mandatory because this is how SAP stores hierarchies in table HRRP_DIRECTORY-HRYID

H109 (Hierarchy type) / HONE (Chart of Accounts) / H003 (Name of Hierarchy)   ---> GL Account Hierarchy

H001 (Name of Hierarchy)  ---> FSV Hierarchy

When i use a FSV Hierarchy 'H001' as the default, the CDS View and KPI returns data.

as select from I_MargAnlysProdAndSrvcCube
              (P_GLAccountHierarchy : 'H001')

From a CDS View perspective both hierarchies H001 and H109/HONE/H003 work without error and return data, so the CDS View is not the issue.

I asked google and AI and they point me at a problem with "slash" / and that it could be interpreted on the HTML side as path separator, which would be wrong, because it is a parameter. I tried to use "%2F" inside the CDS but then no data is returned. But how can i tell the HTML Front End to use %2F - if thats the solution?

I also read something about using SEGW and manually providing mapping for the entity set?

So long story short.

How can i properly setup a KPI based on a custom CDS View when the CDS View uses parameters and one of the parameters is using a slash.

Another questions: Is there no alternative to @odata.publish: true ? Why can´t i use "modern approach" and use an analytical projection view inside the KPI app as data source with InA service ? (On Prem 2023 FPS 1)

Thank you

Regards

David

entry.content:
The question has been already asked in the forum but the solutions were not very clear.

I've got a program which displays an ALV hierarchy via the class CL_GUI_ALV_TREE.

When the user expands a node to see its children nodes, a dump occurs:

  • GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED
  • CL_ALV_TREE_BASE==============CP
  • Field symbol has not been assigned yet.
  • The termination occurred in ABAP program or include "CL_ALV_TREE_BASE==============CP", in  "SET_ITEMS_FOR_COLUMN". The main program was "ZXXXXXXXX". In the source code, the termination point is in line 29 of include "CL_ALV_TREE_BASE==============CM00L".
  • 27 ASSIGN mt_outtab->* TO <tab1>.
  • 28 " assign local copy of initial line of <tab1> to <wa>.
  • >>>>> CREATE DATA l_dref_wa LIKE LINE OF <tab1>.
  • 30 ASSIGN l_dref_wa->* TO <wa>.

By debug, MT_OUTTAB shows the value "->FREED ITAB".

How to fix that?

System: ABAP 7.58 SP 1.

Thanks.

Sandra

entry.title: New Shell Bar in SAP Build Work Zone - What Admins Need to Know
entry.content:
Hello, SAP Build Work Zone enthusiasts! 🌟

We’re excited to share some important updates that may have gone unnoticed during the holiday season—particularly regarding UX consistency-related (UXC) changes designed to enhance your SAP Build Work Zone experience.

Since the December 10, 2025 release, there is a new improved shellbar available with SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, which will eventually become the default for all Work Zone sites. This updated shellbar delivers a more consistent user experience and an enhanced user menu. Additionally it simplifies logo customization in your site’s shell bar without requiring an entirely new theme, thanks to the new Shell Bar customization options!  For more details, please check 🔗 SAP KBA 3673380 .

 

What’s New?

1. Toggle the New Shell Bar Experience

Admins can now choose whether to enable the updated Shell header component on a per-site basis. Here’s how it works:

  • OFF (Default for existing sites until March 2026): Your site will continue rendering as before.
  • ON (Default for new sites and after March 2026): Your site will adopt the new UXC changes, giving you a fresh, modern look!


2. Customize Your Site Header

When UXC changes are turned ON, you can further personalize your site with:

  • Custom Label: Set a unique name for your site header.
  • Custom Logo & Alt Text: Add a link to your own logo for a branded experience.

3. Future-Proof Your Work Zone

  • By end of March 2026: All sites will render with UXC changes by default (but you can still turn them off).
  • By end of June 2026: The toggle will be removed, and all sites will adopt the new UX design.

This ensures alignment with SAP’s latest UX standards while giving you time to prepare.

What to Watch For

If you enable the new Shell Bar, some automation scripts, custom plugins, or SAP Companion hotspots may need adjustments. Here’s what to do: 

✅ Update automation scripts interacting with the DOM. 

✅ Reconfigure custom SAP Companion hotspots if selectors have changed. 

✅ Test custom plugins to ensure they work with the new Shell Bar.

For detailed guidance on how to resolve possible issues, check out: 🔗 SAP Note 3627739. 

Call to Action: Try Out the New Shell Bar Today!

We encourage all customers to turn on the new Shell Bar in a test environment and explore the enhancements. By checking these changes upfront and applying the new settings to all your sites, you can ensure a seamless transition without any disruptions to your productive sites. Ideally, we recommend turning on the new setting for productive sites before the default behavior changes.

Your feedback is invaluable to us. If you encounter any issues or have suggestions, please share your experience or get in touch with us via the comments section.

🚀 Try it now:

  1. Go to Site Settings → Site Branding.
  2. Toggle "Apply New Shell Bar" to ON.  
    If you want, you can customize your logo and label.
  3. Test your scripts, plugins, and workflows.

Let us know how it goes—we’d love to hear your thoughts! 

Stay tuned for more updates, and happy customizing! 🎉

— The SAP Build Work Zone Admin Environment Team

entry.title: Re: CL_GUI_ALV_TREE: dump GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED in SET_ITEMS_FOR_COLUMN of CL_ALV_TREE_BASE
entry.content:
In fact, the questions were answered but there was no confirmation that they were correct, or the questions addressed another problem with almost the same symptom but concerning a method which is not SET_ITEMS_FOR_COLUMN.

The problem is that the internal table LT_OUTTAB passed to IT_OUTTAB is declared locally. It's freed at the end of the method. Instead, declare it globally (or as an attribute of the class and declare the instance as global and don't free the instance).

 

METHOD display.
  DATA lt_outtab TYPE TABLE OF ...
  ...
  CLEAR lt_outtab.
  o_alv_tree->set_table_for_first_display( EXPORTING ...
                                           CHANGING it_outtab = lt_outtab
                                           ... ).
  LOOP AT ...
    o_alv_tree->add_node( ... ).
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

 

Said differently, to make lt_outtab global, move DATA lt_outtab inside a section of the Class Definition or in the Top Include.

entry.title: Re: Just-in-sequence: Sequence Number in POs
entry.content:
Dear @Jana_Hauck,

Yes in Public Cloud you can work with sequence numbers at the operation level in production orders the standard sequence plus parallel and alternative sequences. These sequences are typically defined in the routing and then copied into the production order when it’s created

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/e801b753128eb44ce10000000a174cb4.html

How you work with them in Public Cloud:

  • Display sequences in the order
    • In Display Production Order (CO03) you can show the Sequence Overview (standard + parallel/alternative sequences) and drill into details

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/e8021fefc094432eb038bcd7f3ea98fc.html

  • In Manage Production Orders you can also display parallel and alternative sequences on the order’s detail screen Order Schedule / operations view

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2336')/S36

  • Maintain sequences in the order within allowed scope
    • In Change Production Order (CO02) the Sequence Overview shows standard + parallel/alternative sequences and you can define or delete parallel sequences

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/c448c58b70c344cbb7af9bb6d05279cf.html

  • Read/use sequence data for reporting or integration
    • There is a released CDS view for Production Order Sequence based on table AFFL to retrieve production order sequence data
    • Manufacturing APIs also expose objects around production order operations / routing sequences for example production routing sequence endpoints

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/7b47cbb4384c497b993c670138aec9c7.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d35113ee62644d3abee1aaec148291d9/7cc3f670f45243709dc038ef3ee0a443.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: 06 SAP SuccessFactors HCM implementation - Complex Requirements Process Design with Example- Part 2
entry.content:
This blog walks through the complex requirements design process in SAP SuccessFactors HCM implementation projects. The Complex Requirements Process Design blog series is structured into two parts:

 

  • Part 1: Focuses on the process design framework, supporting tools, governance model, and ownership across functional and technical teams. 

    https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/05-sap-successfactors-hcm-implementation-complex-requirements-process/ba-p/14305678                         

        

  • Part 2: Illustrates the process design approach through a detailed implementation example, highlighting design decisions, dependencies, and technical design. 

 

1. Customer Requirement:

 

 The global customer has been operating SAP S/4HANA HCM (migrated from SAP ERP HCM) for over 21 years, supporting Personnel Administration (PA), Organizational Management (OM), and Payroll for U.S. and Canadian employees. The HCM landscape has evolved over time, and complex customizations have been built to support diverse requirements.  Current system has a complex process to handle the Temporary Assignment (TA) to meet complex North America–specific business and compliance requirements.

As part of the SAP SuccessFactors implementation, PA and OM will transition to Employee Central (EC), integrated with SuccessFactors Talent modules, while Payroll will remain on SAP S/4HANA via EC–Payroll integration. This hybrid deployment introduces a critical design requirement: the existing Temporary Assignment process must be re-engineered in SuccessFactors Employee Central without disrupting S4HANA payroll, Connected, or downstream integrations.

Temporary Assignment refers to a situation where an employee works in a role outside their regular scope for a defined period (minimum of one day), while remaining under their existing employment contract.

 

 2. Requirement analysis :  

 

Understanding the requirements is the first and most critical phase of the design process. This phase focused on gaining a deep understanding of the customer’s existing Temporary Assignment (TA) framework, which—while highly customized—has proven to be stable, scalable, and effective.

To ensure the SuccessFactors design fully supports the current-state behavior, detailed discussions were conducted with process owners, functional leads, and technical teams to analyze how the TA process is currently configured and executed in SAP S/4HANA HCM. The following activities were completed as part of this phase:

  1. End-to-end walkthrough of the current Temporary Assignment process led by the customer, covering both functional and payroll-relevant steps
  2. Collection and review of existing current-state documentation, including process flows, configuration details, and custom logic
  3. Detailed analysis of S/4HANA HCM configuration, identifying standard versus custom components supporting the TA process
  4. Assessment of current challenges and pain points, including operational constraints and system limitations
  5. Identification of dependency items, such as infotypes, workflows, integrations, reporting, and downstream systems
  6. Impact analysis, evaluating how moving PA and OM to Employee Central affects the S4HCM and Time/Benefits Applications process and its payroll touchpoints.

These structured requirements analysis established a baseline for solution design, ensuring that the SuccessFactors implementation preserves existing business outcomes while minimizing risk to payroll and dependent processes.

 

3. Design Considerations and Dependencies: 

 

Following the requirements analysis, a high-level process flow was created to illustrate the key systems, integration points, and business processes involved in the Temporary Assignment requirement. Given the multiple interdependencies across Employee Central, SAP S/4HANA HCM, and downstream processes, it was critical to visualize the end-to-end flow before progressing to detailed solution design.

Each identified dependency was then analyzed individually, with a focus on functional behavior, integration impact, and payroll sensitivity. For every dependency, a detailed design approach was defined to ensure that changes introduced in SuccessFactors Employee Central do not adversely affect existing processes or payroll outcomes.

In collaboration with the customer, a comprehensive requirements and design document was developed covering all functional, technical, and integration aspects of the Temporary Assignment process. This artifact served as a single source of truth, providing full visibility into process dependencies, data ownership, system responsibilities, and impact areas, and enabled informed design decisions for the target-state solution.

 

4. Employee Central and RCM Design :

 

Designing the SAP SuccessFactors solution is a critical aspect of this implementation, as the Temporary Assignment process spans multiple systems and initiation points. The target-state design had to accommodate existing system behavior while ensuring data consistency, process continuity, and payroll integrity. The following key design considerations guided the SuccessFactors solution:

Key Requirement considerations for SF functional design:

 

  • Temporary Assignment initiation from an existing external application
    Temporary Assignments are primarily initiated through an existing external application (TAIA) that includes its own approval processes and workflows. There is no plan to replace this system; therefore, the future-state design ensures that this application continues to function as the system of initiation, with seamless integration into SuccessFactors Employee Central.
  • Temporary Assignment initiation from SuccessFactors Recruiting (Internal Hire scenarios)
    In select scenarios, Temporary Assignments are initiated via SuccessFactors Recruiting Management (RCM) for internal hire rotations. The design accounts for these scenarios by aligning RCM.
  • Temporary Assignment extensions and terminations across systems
    For most scenarios, Temporary Assignment extensions and terminations are managed directly in Employee Central. However, the design also supports cases where these actions are initiated from the external application, requiring bidirectional integration and robust event handling to maintain data synchronization.
  • Identifier generation for existing Temporary Assignment records
    In the SAP S/4HANA system, personnel numbers were not generated for Temporary Assignments. As part of the migration to Employee Central, a strategy was defined to generate unique User IDs and/or Assignment IDs for existing Temporary Assignment Employment records. Post go-live, Employee Central becomes the system of record and will automatically generate these identifiers for all new Temporary Assignments and generate the IDs.

Functional Design to Demo:

 

  • Functional Requirements & Mapping: Created a detailed decision matrix and use-case document to capture and map all functional requirements.
  • EC Design Perspective: From an Employee Central (EC) design point of view, the solution is straightforward as standard EC Temporary Assignment (Higher Duty) functionality supports it.
  • Configuration & Demonstration: Enabled Temporary assignment in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central (SF EC).Demonstrated the configured solution to the customer, showing the end-to-end user experience of how it works in different scenarios.
  • RCM Design: Designed a detailed process for RCM and RCM – EC data sync.

Introduction of MDF Object for  Hold Temporary Assignment Details :

 We have introduced an MDF object to hold Temporary Assignment details. The rationale for creating this object is outlined below:

  • Simplify Interface Complexity
    • Current interfaces contain extremely complex logic.
    • Using the MDF object as a single source of truth reduces complexity and standardizes data handling.
    • External systems no longer need to combine data from multiple employment portlets.
  • Capture Deletions Efficiently
    • Data Hub and other external systems need to be notified when a Temporary Assignment is deleted.
    • Without the MDF object, writing logic to extract deleted records across multiple API calls becomes complex.

 

5. Temporary Assignment Data Migration Approach: 

 

The primary challenge is migrating Temporary Assignment data from S/4HANA and mapping it correctly to the Employee Central (EC) data model.

 

  • Data Migration Scope: Top-of-stack data. Any employee with an active Temporary Assignment and any future-dated Temporary Assignment records were migrated.
  • Data Extraction Logic: We used InfoPorter for S4HANA Data Migration, and a separate template group was created that includes all Temporary Assignment portlets to standardize extraction and transformation rules.
  • Data Migration Dependencies: Active Employment data must be successfully migrated before migrating Temporary Assignment data.
  • Number Range Assignment: Added the 90-series to Personnel Number during migration for User ID and Assignment ID. This ensures migrated records are easily identifiable.   
  •    Temporary Assignment Portlets Sequence: 

6. Technical Integrations Design:

 

 The integrations have the greatest impact, and we need to support existing data contracts across all systems, ensuring no impact on payroll.

 

 7. End-to-End High-Level Design:

 

Below is the overall design implemented, along with a few things that evolved during implementation.

8. Design contributions, implementation, and challenges: 

 

  • Contributions from Solution Architects, Functional Leads, Technical Leads, Technical Architects, Data Migration Architects, and the customer team were critical.

 

  • Certain design aspects were adjusted during implementation. Initially, we planned to sync Temp Assignment Employment Portlet details to MDF through Business/Integration Center. However, the deletion requirement necessitated using a different API, so we implemented it via SAP IS.

 

  • During implementation, we identified that the planned end date could not be updated through the API. We collaborated with the SAP product team to enhance this functionality.

 

9. Conclusion: 

 

  • When designing solutions for any requirement—big or small—the critical factors are that it must not disrupt payroll and that there is minimal or no manual work.
  •  Never underestimate complexity based on high-level requirements. A detailed analysis is essential to capture all requirements and dependencies.
  • Having skilled resources and an open mind to consider different design ideas is key to success.
  • Document the reasoning behind each design decision recommendation. This prevents repeated explanations and saves the team time in the long run.

 

PS: I have generalized the requirement a bit due to the sensitivity of some requirements, and don’t want to reveal customer-specific data. If you have any specific questions or want to learn more, please DM me.

entry.title: Re: The Event-Driven SAP Core: Moving from Request-Response to Real-Time Agility
entry.content:
Excellent post @sachin_soni ! Good to bring awareness of such important topic!

I’ve spent many years in consulting, and there are still plenty of people who don’t fully grasp event-driven architecture. As you mentioned is important that architects explain the pros & cons of such architecture since it introduces a new level of complexity that customers need to understand and plan for; without awareness, the benefits can be undermined by hidden challenges, from integration touchpoints to event orchestration and eventual consistency.

When done thoughtfully, event-driven architectures can unlock greater scalability, responsiveness, and decoupled systems, while still keeping risk in check through disciplined design and proactive validation.

entry.title: Re: WT for replenishment not using preferred unit of measure
entry.content:
Refer KBA Note 2745415 AUoM during Warehouse Task creation.
This is the standard behavior. The Alternative Unit of Measure is determined in the BAdI /SCWM/EX_CORE_RMS_HU_QUAN in this process. Currently, the standard implementation of this BAdI is executed in class /SCWM/CL_EI_CORE_RMS_HU_QUAN. This standard implementation always sets the Alternative Unit of Measure to the BUoM, if no bigger AUoM is found.

If product master data is set as 10 PC = 1 PAL, and the request is 10 PC quantity, system will determine AUoM as 1 PAL, since this is the bigger UoM.

In case the bigger AUoM do not result in an integer conversion, then this UoM will not be used, as the coding perform a check if the division of AUoM/BUoM conversion is perfect (no rest left from the operation)..

Also i would recommend to check SAP Note 3543691 - Wrong AUoM during WT creation, in case you still see WT created with decimals.

entry.title: Question: Role-based access control in a chatbot using SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I’m preparing a pre-sales presentation for a real use case with SAP Generative AI Hub, and I want to address a security topic that customers typically ask about.

I have been searching for quite a long time for official documentation, concrete examples, or direct answers on this, but I have not been able to find a single clear reference that explains it conclusively.

My question is conceptual: when a chatbot consumes a model via Generative AI Hub and needs to retrieve business information (for example from SAP S/4HANA), how can we ensure that the responses respect the end user’s roles and authorization levels, and that no information is exposed beyond what the user is allowed to access?

I would especially appreciate:

  • Official SAP references (documentation, guides, or notes) that describe the recommended approach for access control and governance in these scenarios.

  • Expert recommendations on how to explain, at a high level, that end-to-end user authorizations are preserved (for example concepts such as identity/principal propagation), without going into configuration details.

Thank you for any guidance or references you can share!

PD:
I've done https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/solving-business-problems-using-sap-s-generative-ai-hub
And still, i'm not 100% sure on how roles,governance and security works.

entry.title: Re: Is freight order confirmation Mandatory for Freight T...
entry.content:
Hello @PingZhang ,

From a technical API perspective, Order Confirmation is not a strict prerequisite for a carrier to report events via the Provider API for Order Events, provided that the carrier has received a valid Tracking Request.

Here is the detailed breakdown of why this is the case for your "Freight Tracking Only" scope:

1. Separation of Processes (Subcontracting vs. Tracking)
In BN4L, Freight Subcontracting (sending the Freight Order and receiving Confirmation) and Freight Tracking (sending a Tracking Request and receiving Events) are handled by separate services and interfaces.


• Subcontracting: Uses the Provider API for Freight Subcontracting (for the order) and Consumer API for Freight Subcontracting (for confirmation).


• Tracking: Uses the Consumer API for Order Tracking (to send the request to the carrier) and the Provider API for Order Events (for the carrier to send events).


Because these are decoupled, if your integration scope is Tracking Only, the carrier’s ability to report events depends on the Tracking Request, not the Order Confirmation.

2. The Tracking Request is the Technical Trigger
For the carrier to post an event via API, they simply need the valid identifiers (such as the Freight Order ID, Bill of Lading, or a specific Tracking ID) to match the event to the document in the network.

• How they get the ID: The Shipper sends a tracking request (technically the TransportationOrderGenericTrackedProcessRequest from SAP TM) to the network. This triggers a "Put Order for Tracking" message to the carrier's system.


• Result: Once the carrier receives this payload containing the IDs, they can immediately POST events back to BN4L using those IDs. The system validates the event against the existence of the freight order/tracked process, not its confirmation status.


3. Contrast with Manual (UI) Process
You correctly noted that the manual process often requires confirmation. This is largely a UI workflow constraint rather than a backend API constraint:

• Manual UI: In the standard portal flow, a Freight Order often sits in the Freight Orders for Confirmation app first. Once confirmed, it moves to the Freight Orders for Reporting (or Manage Freight Execution) app, enabling the "Report Event" buttons,.


• SAP TM Logic: In the backend SAP TM system, the "Tracking Request" (which enables the API flow) is typically triggered when the order status is set to "In Execution" or "Ready for Transportation Execution".

◦ If the shipper configures TM to trigger tracking independently of the confirmation step (or if the shipper manually sets the status to "In Execution"), the Tracking Request is sent, and the carrier can report events via API immediately.




IN SUMMARY:
For your API integration scenario:
1. Shipper sends the Freight Order (FO) to BN4L.
2. Shipper triggers the Tracking Request (based on status "In Execution" in TM).
3. Carrier receives the Tracking Request (via API) containing the traceID or altKey.
4. Carrier POSTs events using the Provider API for Order Events.
The carrier does not need to send an API Order Confirmation to perform step 4.

entry.title: BW-Like Authorizations Multiple-Dim DAC vs Single-Dim DACs
entry.content:
One Multiple-Dimensions DAC or Multiple Single-Dimension DACs
Basically
If the Restriction is equal, then the DAC considers the Criterion entries as an "AND"
If the Restriction is not-equal, then the DAC considers the Criterion entries as "OR"

Use Case:
We have multiple RSIOBJNM-like f.e.: 0PLANT, 0COMP_CODE and we would like to implement RSINFOCUBE-like access as well.


One Multiple Dimensions DAC: 
In DSP it is not mandatory to have all Criterions used in DAC populated. Enough is one Restriction/Criterion row.

Example:
User has maintained access to 0PLANT, but 0COMP_CODE is missing.
DSP: User will see maintained PLANTS and ALL COMP_CODEs
BW: User will get "No authorizations" message

DSP does not check all Restriction columns used in DAC, like BW does. If a User has access to one Dimension, user has access to * for all other Dimensions used in the DAC.

If the source for the authorization view/table is BW (loaded regularly from BW system into a secure DSP space), then we always should have both dimensions correctly populated. But in case not, we bypass security.


Multiple Single-Dimension DACs:
Better approach is to use multiple DACs --> implicit "AND", with the mandatory requirement to have at least one Restriction/Criterion row, all DACs must have the user populated, else nothing will be shown.
Example:
User has maintained access to 0PLANT, but 0COMP_CODE is missing.
DSP: User will get "No authorizations" message
BW: User will get "No authorizations" message

We implicitly ensure, that the user has to be authorizes for all in DAC used Dimension. BW-Like behavior.

 

How-to simulate Multiple RSIOBJNM (compounded IOs as well):
Create Multiple (Single Dimension) DACs for each RSIOBJNM
Implicit "AND" ensures, that security will always fail in case the user has no values assigned to DAC relevant Dimension.

How-to simulate RSINFOCUBE with RSIOBJNM:
Create Multiple (Single Dimension) DACs for each RSIOBJNM and RSINFOCUBE
For the RSINFOCUBE: create a new dimension in each DAC relevant View, simulating 0PROVIDER. Assign PROVIDER_DAC to the new dimension.
How-to simulate RSINFOAREA:
Visibility cannot be adjusted withing a Space, but if RSINFOCUBE-like access is implemented, the users will not be able to access the data anyway.


Possible use-cases for Multiple-Dimensions DAC :
MasterData based DACs: Based on single Master Data objects, for example: all of it's attributes can be moved into single DAC. Easier maintenance/support. User is not expected to be for each MD-Attribute populated. 

Touples Dimensions based DACs: two different Dimensions (same master data/different view of the data) from the same source field

Nice new feature in DSP(RSECADMIN Run-As): Now possible, however only in Views (not in Models / AMs yet).

 

 

Initial BW-Sec to DSP-Sec Blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/bw-like-authorizations-in-datasphere-dsp/ba-p/14153918

Main Blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/bw-vs-datasphere-dsp-amp-sac/ba-p/1428...

entry.title: SuccessFactors Learning - Competency Integration with Learning: Reporting & Management Capabilities
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently supporting clients with the integration of competencies into SAP SuccessFactors Learning. We understand that competencies are not created directly within Learning, but rather need to be brought in via the connector, Capabilities, or Attribute APM.

We are specifically seeking clarity on how competencies and skills are managed once they are integrated and associated with learning items. In our demo environments, we have been testing various scenarios ahead of client calls, but have encountered several issues:

  • Most competency-related standard reports are not working or are displaying blank results.
  • There is very limited documentation available on current competency management features in SFLMS.
  • The "Competency Data" report provides only basic information (e.g., imported competencies, rating scales), but does not show which learning courses are associated with which competencies, or which users have achieved certain competencies through course completion.
  • There are 9 standard LMS reports for competencies in our demo instance, but almost none of them function as expected. Are these legacy reports that are no longer supported?

Given these challenges, we are trying to understand:

  1. Which competency-related features and reports are still supported in the current SFLMS?
  2. Are Competency Profiles still applicable and actively used in Learning?
  3. What is the recommended way to manage and report on the relationship between learning courses, competencies, and proficiency levels achieved by employees, if not through the standard LMS reports?
  4. Is there any up-to-date documentation or guidance on best practices for competency management and analytics in SFLMS?

This feels like a significant gap in the integration, as the lack of reporting and analytics impacts the value of integrating competencies with Learning.

Any insights, documentation, or experiences from the community would be greatly appreciated, as we want to ensure we are providing the best possible guidance to our clients.

Thank you in advance!

ご担当者様

How to change the default UTC timezone in email notification  to JP

entry.title: Re: Cloud ALMの通知メールがUCT時間で表示される
entry.content:
ご担当者様

平素よりお世話になっております。
2024年10月16日以降、Intelligent Event Processingにおいて、メールおよびチャット通知のタイムゾーンを設定することが可能になりました。
変更方法につき、KBA3337151 に記載した下記Resolution部分をご確認いただけますと幸いです。

3337151 -SAP Cloud ALM for Operations alert e-mails appear delayed when comparing alert/metric timestamps with value in the source service/system
----------------------------------------------------------
Since Oct 16, 2024 it is possible to configure the Time Zone for Email and Chat Notifications in Intelligent Event Processing.

You can specify the time zone for email and chat notifications. When configured, the reporting time stamps in the notifications for Send Email to and Send Chat Notification event actions are displayed in the configured time zone.

To configure the time zone,
1.Open the Intelligent Event Processing app.
2.Navigate to Configuration  Application Settings  Time Zone.
3.Select the preferred time zone in the Time Zone for Email and Chat Notifications dropdown.
4.Choose Save.
----------------------------------------------------------
以上です。宜しくお願い致します。
SAPサポート

entry.title: Solution Manager 暗号アルゴリズムの利用の有無について
entry.content:
SAP Solman環境で以下の暗号アルゴリズムの利用をされているかご教示いただけますでしょうか。
また確認が必要であればその方法も教えていただけますでしょうか。

 1/28(水)を目途にご回答をお願い致します。
対象のアルゴリズムは以下となります。用途: 
公開鍵暗号（鍵交換） 
暗号アルゴリズム: 
PSEC-KEM224
RSA-KEM2048
RSAOAEP2048
ECDHE224
DHE2048用途:
デジタル署名 
暗号アルゴリズム: 
ECAO224
ESIGN-PSS2112
RSA-PSS2048
ECDSA224
RSA PKCS#1 v1.ZIPCODE
DSA2048用途:
ハッシュ関数
暗号アルゴリズム:
SHA-224用途:
メッセージ認証コード
暗号アルゴリズム: 
HMAC-SHA1
HMAC-RIPEMD-180
HMAC-MD5

以上、よろしくお願いいたします。


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Solution Manager 暗号アルゴリズムの利用の有無について
entry.content:
SAP Solman製品の暗号アルゴリズム関連情報は下記ノート抜粋をご参照お願いします。

BC-SEC-SSL-CFG
510007 - Additional considerations about setting up SSL on Application Server ABAP
「SAP Solution Manager システム 7.[012] の場合は、代わりに ssl/client_ciphersuites に以下の値を使用してください。

        ssl/client_ciphersuites = 912:PFS:HIGH::EC_X25519:EC_P256:EC_HIGH

トランザクション SOLMAN_SETUP のバグおよび 2 つの TX STC01 タスクリスト "SAP_BASIS_CONFIG_OSS_COMM" および "SUPPORT_HUB_CONFIG" のバグを回避します。これにより、パラメータ ssl/client_ciphersuites の推奨パラメータフラグ値 150 が誤って解釈されます。

上述の推奨の暗号スイートパラメータ値により、TLSv1.2+TLSv1.1+TLSv1.0、PFS (Perfect Forward Secrecy) 暗号スイートのサポート、送信 SSL/TLS で保護された通信のクライアント証明書のブラインド送信、および破棄可能な RC4 ベースの TLS 暗号スイート (クラス MEDIUM) が有効化されます。 CommonCryptoLib 8.5.4 (2016 年 9 月) 以降では、暗号スイート 3DES_EDE_CBC がクラス HIGH からクラス MEDIUM に降格され、3DES および RC4 暗号スイートの両方が上述の推奨パラメータ値によって無効化されます。 CommonCryptoLib 8.5.42 (2022 年 2 月) 以降では、暗号スイートクラス MEDIUM が *空白* になり、3DES および RC4 暗号化の TLS 暗号スイートでは明示的なオプトイン "e3DES" および "eRC4" が必要です。このセクションの後半でサンプルパラメータ値を参照してください。

ssl/client_ciphersuites および ssl/ciphersuites の推奨パラメータ値は、8.4.31 より前の CommonCryptoLib および先行ライブラリ SAPCRYPTOLIB 5.5.5 を含め、既存のすべてのライブラリバージョンに対して最適な機能を提供します。 実際の相互運用性の問題を簡単に設定しながら、セキュリティ上のメリットを得ることができるため、独自のカスタムプロトコルバージョンおよび暗号スイートをローリングするのではなく、上記の値を使用することを強くお奨めします。」

entry.title: Select ship to party in outbound delivery creation in subcontracting process
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We have the following case:

In the subcontracting process, the supplier have different locations to perform the assembly. 

The supplier of the subcontracting process have assigned a customer with different ship to party partner functions. Each of ship to party customer corresponds to one of the supplier locations.

When we create the outbound delivery to send the components to the supplier, the outbound delivery is created with the customer associated to the supplier as a SHIP TO PARTY of the outbound delivery created.

We want to select the ship to party when we create the outbound delivery to assign the correct delivery address in the outbound delivery.

How can we do it?

 

Thanks for your support

entry.title: How Employee Absence date recalculation will work in Successfactors time off
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

Could you please help us to check is it possible to do the Employee Absence date recalculation.

For Example:

Employee 'A' is having 10 days as annual Entitlement for the year 2024 and the bookable period is 01-01-2024 to 31-12-2025.

and 10 days as annual Entitlement for the year 2024 and the bookable period is 01-01-2025 to 31-12-2026.

Employee booked booked all 10 days leave in 2024 year bucket and he booked some days around 6 days in 2025 also. he is having 4 days in 2026 leave bucket.

After that he cancelled 2 days leave in 2024 bucket which is booked for the dates in 2025. So the leave bucket has 2 days for 2024.

After that employee not booked any leaves. So Is it possible to do Absence date recalculation/reallocation to use the leaves which is already deducted from 2025 bucket to 2024 bucket.

Please Let me know. Thanks

Thanks
Murugan Perumal
